self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can stop stale Architecture bounded-result wording from overruling the stronger live currentHead on advanced Architecture chains.
self_improvement.mechanism: The adopted slice downgrades artifact-local next-step wording for stale Architecture bounded-result artifacts once currentHead has advanced elsewhere.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this DEEP slice, the shared resolver still advertised the old bounded-result local next step even after currentHead had already moved to a reopened or later downstream Architecture artifact.
self_improvement.expected_effect: Operators inspecting stale Architecture bounded-result artifacts will be redirected to the live currentHead instead of being shown a no-longer-satisfiable local next step.

decision.rationale: The slice is small, shared, and directly aligns artifact-local wording with currentHead truth without rewriting the integrity model.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
